interactions.summary: Jiang answers the Russia question by saying Russia, not China, is America's defining adversary because it has resources, political will, and territorial integrity to challenge hegemony. The Iran war counters Russia's possible Heartland control after Ukraine; the next twenty years will be Russia versus America, with many countries aligning with Russia as America becomes the Athens-like aggressor.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_01 is Jiang closing the interview; no further interviewer answer appears in the focus refs.

claims.claim: Jiang says America's main adversary is really Russia, not China, because Russia has the resources, political will, and territorial integrity to challenge American hegemony.
He says the Iran war is seen as a way to counter Russia's aggression in Ukraine and possible control over the Heartland.
Jiang predicts the defining struggle of the next twenty years will be between Russia and America rather than the United States and China.
He uses the Peloponnesian War analogy to argue that many countries will align with Russia because America is increasingly seen as the great aggressor, like Athens.
claims.temporal_scope: Historical analogy and alignment prediction on 2026-04-13.

claims.claim: Jiang says Russia cannot realistically defeat America at sea but has no choice but to challenge American sea power.

signature_moments.moment: The apparent China-centered conflict is reclassified as a twenty-year Russia-America struggle.
signature_moments.source_phrase: not between the United States and China but between Russia and America
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the closing reversal that reorganizes the whole Iran/petrodollar discussion around Heartland power.

signature_moments.moment: America becomes Athens: the aggressor against whom the world starts to align.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The analogy gives Jiang's prediction of global alignment a classical historical frame.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The Eurasian strategic space whose possible Russian control Jiang treats as the deeper reason America attacks Iran.
Jiang's analogy in which America plays Athens, the perceived aggressor that drives other states into alignment against it.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-13 Jiang predicts the defining global struggle for roughly the next twenty years will be Russia versus America, not U.S.-China rivalry.

uncertainty_notes.note: The opening phrase says 'main adversary is that China ... it's really Russia'; context shows a correction from the common China frame to Jiang's Russia frame.
